pattern: Comparing dot-separated revisions as integers, padding the shorter version with zeros, decides ordering without string pitfalls like leading zeros.
hints: Each chunk between dots is a number, so '01' and '1' are equal; compare values, not characters.
Walk the revisions left to right; a missing revision on either side counts as zero.
Split both strings on dots, parse each piece as an integer, pad the shorter list with zeros, and return the sign of the first differing pair.

edge_inputs.description: extra trailing zero revisions are equal
longer version with a nonzero tail wins
many revisions, difference deep in the list

stress.description: random multi-revision versions with leading zeros and varying lengths
stress.note: stays within 32-bit revisions and the length cap
